name: Villages of Sobrarbe: Abizanda and Naval — return via the Sierra de Olsón
name_original: Pueblos del  Sobrarbe: Abizanda y Naval — vuelta por la Sierra de Olsón
region_intro: Touring cycling around Abizanda is characterized by its location within the Aragonese Pyrenees, offering diverse landscapes. The region features deep canyons and gorges carved by rivers like the Alcanadre and Vero, notably within the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park. Mountainous terrain, including the Sierra de Balcés and Tozal de Guara, provides challenging ascents, while picturesque river valleys offer varied scenery. The area's geology creates a rugged backdrop for cycling routes. Best touring cycling routes around Abizanda The most popular touring cycling route is Villages of Sobrarbe: Abizanda and Naval — return via the Sierra de Olsón, a 31.2 miles (50.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores local villages and the Sierra de Olsón. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Sierra de Trillo loop from Picatiecho, a difficult 24.8 miles (39.9 km) path. This route offers significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Abizanda – Naval loop from Abizanda, a 17.7 miles (28.5 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 3 hours 2 minutes. Touring cycling around Abizanda is defined by deep canyons, mountainous sierras, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, though most routes are considered difficult. The routes in Abizanda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 370 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Abizanda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Abizanda?
A: There are 10 touring cycling routes available around Abizanda on komoot, offering diverse experiences through the Aragonese Pyrenees. Most of these routes are considered difficult, providing a challenge for experienced cyclists.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Abizanda?
A: Touring cycling around Abizanda is generally challenging. Out of the 10 routes available, 9 are rated as difficult, and 1 is moderate. This indicates that most routes require good fitness and experience, often featuring significant elevation gains through mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Abizanda?
A: While most routes in Abizanda are rated difficult, the broader Sierra de Guara Natural Park, which encompasses Abizanda, does offer some family-friendly cycling options. These routes are typically designed to be less strenuous, allowing for enjoyable rides with children. It's advisable to check individual route details for specific suitability.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on a touring cycling route in Abizanda?
A: Abizanda's touring cycling routes showcase the dramatic and diverse landscapes of the Aragonese Pyrenees. You'll encounter deep canyons and gorges carved by rivers like the Alcanadre and Vero, especially within the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park. Routes also traverse mountainous terrain, including the Sierra de Balcés and Tozal de Guara, as well as picturesque river valleys and agricultural land, sometimes featuring vineyards.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Abizanda?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Trillo loop from Picatiecho&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 24.8-mile (39.9 km) path with significant elevation gain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abizanda – Naval loop from Abizanda&lt;/a&gt;, a 17.7-mile (28.5 km) trail through the local countryside.

Q: What are some interesting natural attractions or viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region offers stunning natural attractions. You can find breathtaking viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5284340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Torreciudad Sanctuary and the Grado I reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7584863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Cotón Trail and Reservoir View&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/961906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vero Gorge&lt;/a&gt; is another spectacular natural monument, and you might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3363730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentes de Lecina&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Abizanda?
A: The touring cycling routes in Abizanda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the wild landscapes, ancient paths connecting villages, and breathtaking viewpoints like the Mirador del Vero, which offer direct immersion in nature.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Abizanda?
A: The Aragonese Pyrenees, including Abizanda, are best explored for touring cycling during spring and autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures and more stable weather conditions compared to the intense heat of summer or the colder, potentially snowy conditions of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any specific regulations or permits required for cycling in the Sierra de Guara Natural Park?
A: The Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park is a protected area. While general cycling on established routes and roads is usually permitted, it's always advisable to check the official regulations of the Natural Park for any specific restrictions, especially concerning off-trail cycling or sensitive areas. Information can typically be found on the park's official website or at local visitor centers.

Q: Is Abizanda well-equipped with amenities like cafes or accommodation for cyclists?
A: Abizanda and its surrounding villages, such as Alquézar, are charming and offer amenities for visitors. You can expect to find cafes, restaurants, and various accommodation options that cater to tourists and outdoor enthusiasts. It's a good idea to check availability and opening hours, especially outside of peak season.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Abizanda?
A: While the region is great for outdoor activities, regulations regarding dogs on cycling routes, especially within the Sierra y Cañones de Guara Natural Park, can vary. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety on shared paths. Always check specific route guidelines or park rules regarding pets.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Abizanda?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the village of Abizanda, as well as at common starting points for popular routes within the Sierra de Guara. Look for designated parking areas or inquire locally upon arrival. Some routes might start from smaller hamlets where parking could be limited, so planning ahead is recommended.
tips: Gravel road that takes you through the two towns crossing the center of Suelves. The ascent is harder than the descent even though it is shorter. The Sierra de Olsón is crossed.

This town, once an essential crossing point in the connection between Somontano and Sobrabre and France, has been known for its salt mines since Muslim times. It therefore has some interesting salt warehouses or "alfolíes".
The 40-meter-high rectangular donchón tower is one of the most unique elements of this town, which draws attention even from afar. The entire population grew as a result of the castle.
